summary: Justin Brennan discusses multiple ways to invest in real estate, using his first 4-unit deal in San Diego ($425K purchase, $1.1M sale after 6 years) as an example of the DIY approach where he controlled everything as broker, property manager, and operator. He emphasizes that while full control is a benefit, self-funding has limits and recommends combining DIY deals with syndication and partnerships to scale.
takeaways: Buy your first deals yourself to maintain 100% control
Start with your own investment capital plus debt financing
Combine DIY deals with syndication/partnerships to scale beyond personal capital limits
Use early DIY wins (e.g., $425K→$1.1M in 6 years) to fund out-of-state syndication
Don't rely solely on full control—diversify through partnerships and crowdfunding sources

skill_candidates.change: Establish yourself as DIY operator (broker + property manager + capital provider) on first deal
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin's first 4-unit deal where he personally served as both broker and property manager while contributing capital, resulting in successful 2.6x return

skill_candidates.change: Validate deal economics through personal operation before scaling
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin ran operations himself on first deal, enabling confident decision to scale via syndication after 6-year hold that turned $425K purchase into $1.1M sale

title: Lesson 1 - Do It Yourself
module: Module 7 - Ways To Invest In Apartments
